title: Highest temperature in London on June 28?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 28 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the London City Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Met Office guidance points to a 26°C maximum in London on June 28 as the most probable outcome, following the recent extreme heatwave that peaked mid-week with widespread 35–38°C readings. A shift toward cooler Atlantic air and increased cloud cover is expected to moderate temperatures over the weekend, aligning with ensemble model consensus that places the highest likelihood near 25–27°C. Historical June averages hover around 20–22°C, underscoring the lingering warmth, yet forecasters note typical uncertainties from local urban heat islands and small shifts in steering patterns could nudge the peak slightly higher or lower. Upcoming model updates and official briefings this weekend will provide the key data releases influencing final trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**A severe early-summer heatwave peaked across France mid-week, driving Paris temperatures to 39–40 °C on June 24–25 before a gradual easing began.** By June 26, official guidance from Météo-France pointed to moderating conditions over the weekend, with Atlantic air and increasing thunderstorm risk expected to cap daytime maxima on June 28. Trader consensus clusters tightly around 30–32 °C (roughly 78 % combined implied probability), reflecting model agreement on cooling yet residual uncertainty over the precise peak.

Key scientific differentiators include the exact arrival and depth of the cooler maritime flow, the timing and coverage of convective clouds or showers (which can suppress insolation and lower highs by 2–4 °C), and small spread among high-resolution runs for the Paris basin. Historical June maxima in the capital average near 25 °C, so even a moderated 31–32 °C day would remain well above normal. With resolution only 48 hours away, the next official forecast updates and any late-model shifts in steering or instability will be the primary drivers of further market movement.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wellington’s winter maximum temperatures are shaped by its exposed Cook Strait location, where frequent southerly flows and strong winds advect cooler maritime air, typically capping daily highs near the long-term June average of 13–14 °C. Current MetService guidance and numerical models indicate mostly cloudy conditions with a chance of showers and easing southerlies for 28 June, supporting a most-likely maximum of 11–12 °C. Seasonal outlooks from NIWA place air temperatures near average for the region, while short-range ensemble spreads remain narrow around these values. Trader consensus, reflected in the 48 % probability on 12 °C and 24 % on 11 °C, aligns with this forecast consensus and the limited potential for warmer northerly advection before the date. Updated model runs or any late shift in wind direction could still adjust the exact bucket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts from sources including AccuWeather and the Israel Meteorological Service point to daytime highs of 29–30°C in Tel Aviv on June 28 under persistent high pressure, clear skies, and light to moderate westerly winds typical of early-summer Mediterranean conditions. These patterns limit cloud cover and allow strong solar heating of the coastal plain while sea breezes provide modest moderation, keeping most ensemble guidance clustered near 30–31°C. Trader consensus favors 31°C because short-range models show limited spread around these values, though minor upward revisions remain possible if sea-breeze timing or localized heating strengthens. Historical June averages near 29–30°C and the absence of significant synoptic changes in the past week reinforce this narrow probability band ahead of final model updates on June 27.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current Japan Meteorological Agency guidance indicates mostly cloudy skies over Tokyo on June 28 amid the early-summer rainy season, limiting solar heating and supporting highs near 24–25 °C. Seasonal outlooks from the same agency project above-normal temperatures through the period due to a strengthened Pacific High, yet short-range model consensus favors modest cooling from increased cloud cover and possible weak frontal passages. These conditions differentiate the closely matched leading outcomes, as even modest shifts in insolation, boundary-layer moisture, or steering flow can alter peak readings by 1–2 °C. Updated model runs and JMA briefings over the next 48 hours will likely drive further market movement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent official forecasts from the Hong Kong Observatory indicate daytime highs of 27–31°C for June 28, with high humidity and a strong chance of showers, aligning closely with the market's leading outcomes of 30°C and 31°C. Above-normal temperatures expected for June–August per long-term HKO guidance further bolster this cluster, while model consensus shows limited risk of extremes above 32°C or below 29°C absent major atmospheric shifts. Updated forecasts in the coming days could refine these implied odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current numerical weather prediction ensembles show Shanghai's June 28 high most likely near 27–28 °C, with the tight spread between these outcomes reflecting modest differences in simulated cloud cover, boundary-layer mixing, and steering flow from the western Pacific subtropical high during the late Meiyu period. eventMetadata.context_description: Singapore's equatorial location and ongoing Southwest Monsoon drive the market's focus on 32°C as the most probable daily maximum for June 28, with 33°C and 31°C close behind. Persistent southeasterly to southwesterly winds promote afternoon thundery showers that typically cap peak heating through increased cloud cover and evaporative cooling, aligning with National Environment Agency outlooks for maximum temperatures of 33–34°C on most June days. Historical June averages hover near 31–32°C, and recent model guidance indicates above-average rainfall for the period, reducing the likelihood of clearer conditions that could push readings to 34°C or higher. Traders are monitoring updated NEA and regional forecasts for any shifts in shower timing or monsoon strength ahead of the date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest AEMET and supporting model guidance position Madrid’s June 28 maximum near 35–36 °C, with the Retiro station as the official reference. Updated runs from AEMET and ECMWF over the next 24–48 hours will be the key catalysts for any repricing ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Central Weather Administration outlooks and extended model guidance point to normal-to-above-average June temperatures across northern Taiwan, with trader probabilities clustered around 29–32 °C reflecting uncertainty in cloud cover and afternoon convection expected for June 28. Taipei’s subtropical monsoon climate typically produces daily highs near 31 °C, but scattered showers or thunderstorms—common in late June—can suppress peak readings by 1–2 °C through reduced insolation, while clearer intervals allow brief spikes toward 33 °C. Ensemble spreads from global models remain wide due to the short lead time and variable steering flow, keeping the market-implied distribution tight between the 29 °C and 32 °C bins. Updated CWA bulletins and local radar trends over the next 48 hours will likely narrow the range as precipitation timing becomes clearer.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in this market centers on a peak of 25–26°C for Chongqing on June 28, with those outcomes together capturing nearly half the probability. This positioning aligns with regional model guidance showing moderating daytime highs driven by increased cloud cover and scattered rainfall associated with the East Asian monsoon’s northward progression. Subtropical moisture and orographic lift from surrounding terrain often suppress maximum temperatures below seasonal climatology of 30–32°C, while urban heat-island effects in the city core add modest warming that keeps values above 24°C. Ensemble spreads remain wide because small shifts in timing or intensity of convective activity can alter the daily maximum by 2–3°C. Updated runs from the China Meteorological Administration and global models expected in the next 24–48 hours will likely refine these probabilities ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast guidance from European models shows Amsterdam highs on June 28 likely peaking near 25–26 °C under a moderating northwest flow, with increased cloud cover and scattered drizzle limiting afternoon solar heating after several days of anomalous warmth. Persistent high pressure that drove temperatures above 30 °C earlier in the week is shifting eastward, allowing cooler maritime air to advect across the North Sea while surface winds strengthen to 15–20 km/h. Ensemble spreads remain narrow around the 25–27 °C range, reflecting modest uncertainty in exact cloud timing and boundary-layer mixing rather than large-scale pattern shifts. Market-implied odds cluster tightly on 25–26 °C because these values align with the consensus maximum from the latest deterministic and ensemble runs, with lower probabilities assigned to 27 °C or higher only if unexpected clearing occurs. Traders monitor the next model cycles and KNMI updates for any revisions to boundary-layer moisture or wind direction that could alter peak readings by a degree or two.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders are pricing the June 28 high temperature in Kuala Lumpur most heavily around 32–34 °C because June climatology and current model guidance point to typical southwest-monsoon conditions. Long-term averages show daytime maxima near 32–33 °C, with daily ranges of 31.7–34.4 °C expected this month according to extended forecasts. Short-range models indicate moderate cloud cover and scattered convection that should cap peak heating while humidity remains very high, keeping values from climbing much above 34 °C or dropping below 32 °C. Urban heat-island effects in the city center add slight upward bias, yet no strong ridging or clear-sky anomalies appear imminent to push extremes. Resolution will hinge on the official Malaysian Meteorological Department observation at the reference station, where even small differences in afternoon insolation or timing of showers can shift the daily maximum by 1–2 °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather prediction ensembles indicate a narrow band of possible daily maximum temperatures for Guangzhou on June 28, reflected in the closely matched market-implied odds peaking at 32°C. Subtropical monsoon dynamics, including variable afternoon convection, cloud cover, and onshore flow from the South China Sea, create the primary forecast uncertainty. Typical late-June climatology features highs near 32°C, though the urban heat island effect in this densely built coastal city can add 1–2°C to daytime peaks under clear skies. Divergent model solutions on precipitation timing and wind strength differentiate the 31–34°C outcomes, with new guidance from the China Meteorological Administration expected to refine probabilities before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Current short-range forecasts for Qingdao on June 28 point to a daily maximum near 26°C under cloudy skies with light winds and possible showers, keeping the market’s closely bunched 29–31 °C outcomes driven by model spread rather than a clear warming signal.** Qingdao’s Yellow Sea location produces a strong marine influence that caps daytime heating through sea breezes and higher humidity, while late-June climatology favors highs of 25–28 °C. Recent model runs show limited diurnal warming potential because of persistent cloud cover and weak southerly flow; any break in clouds or drier air could push readings 2–3 °C higher, which explains why traders assign roughly equal weight to the top three bins. Updated guidance from regional models and the next 48-hour observational cycle will be the key catalysts for shifts in implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent PAGASA guidance for Metro Manila projects a June 28 high near 32 °C with 90–100 % rain chance, reflecting the southwest monsoon’s onset and widespread convective cloud cover that limits solar heating. Ensemble models show modest spread around 31–33 °C depending on the timing and intensity of afternoon showers, while historical June maxima average 32 °C under similar humidity and wind regimes. Trader consensus clusters on these values because official observations use standardized maximum-temperature criteria at reference stations, and any brief clearing could push readings 1 °C higher before renewed monsoon activity suppresses further warming.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing heatwave conditions across northern India, with recent Lucknow maxima near 41°C and IMD forecasts pointing to 40–42°C amid above-normal temperatures, drive the market's clustering around 39–41°C. Key variables include the timing of monsoon advance, which could introduce cloud cover and reduce peaks, versus persistent clear skies and dry westerlies sustaining highs; local factors like humidity, wind speed, and urban heat island effects add variability. Short-term model runs from IMD and global ensembles show divergence on exact intensification, while historical June baselines around 37°C highlight the current anomaly. Traders weigh these against potential rapid shifts from updated 48-hour forecasts ahead of June 28 resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast guidance from global models points to a daily maximum near 12–13°C in Buenos Aires on June 28, consistent with the market’s leading outcomes at 32% and 23.5% implied probability. Winter conditions feature a high-pressure ridge promoting light southerly flow that restricts daytime heating while enabling overnight radiational cooling. Limited insolation at this latitude, combined with potential patchy cloud cover or slight wind shifts, creates narrow variability around the seasonal average high of 15–16°C early in the month. Model consensus on exact peak temperature remains tight yet sensitive to small changes in boundary-layer moisture or steering patterns, sustaining the close spread across 11–14°C bins. Updated short-range runs expected within the next 12–24 hours will refine the resolution threshold.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent short-term forecasts from Environment Canada and The Weather Network indicate a high near 23–25°C for Toronto on June 28, 2026, consistent with climatological norms around 24–25°C at Pearson Airport, though the market’s clustering around 27–28°C reflects trader weighting of the broader seasonal outlook for above-normal temperatures. Minor model variations in boundary-layer moisture, timing of any weak trough, or afternoon insolation could readily shift the observed high across the tightly contested 26–28°C range, explaining the balanced probabilities as traders incorporate the latest official guidance ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model runs point to a building subtropical ridge over the Southeast driving Atlanta’s June 28 maximum into the low-to-mid 90s, with dew points in the low-to-mid 70s supporting heat indices near or above 100 °F. Scattered afternoon convection tied to weak shortwave energy and modest mid-level moisture could cap surface heating and shave a degree or two from the daily peak, keeping the distribution centered on the 90–93 °F bins that together command over two-thirds of market-implied probability. Historical June climatology shows a 93 °F normal high, but recent soil moisture from prior rains and variable boundary-layer mixing introduce the narrow spread between the leading 92–93 °F (38.5 %) and 90–91 °F (29.0 %) outcomes. Updated model cycles and the 18Z NWS forecast discussion expected later today will be the next catalysts for any shift in trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model consensus indicate Miami highs near 90–92°F on June 28, with afternoon heating under partly cloudy skies and scattered thunderstorm chances that could cap the peak depending on timing and coverage. Typical June climatology features mean highs around 89–90°F, but persistent ridging and modest moisture return support readings at or slightly above average, keeping the 90–91°F and 92–93°F bins tightly matched in trader pricing. Sea-breeze convergence, wind direction, and convective initiation remain the key variables that could shift the observed maximum by 1–2°F, with updated NWS short-range forecasts and next model cycles expected to refine probabilities ahead of the daily high.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 28 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the William P. Hobby Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent National Weather Service and AccuWeather guidance places Houston’s June 28 high in the low-to-mid 90s under persistent subtropical high pressure, light southerly flow off the Gulf, and mostly sunny skies typical of late June climatology. This setup favors 92–93 °F as the most probable peak, with 94–95 °F remaining plausible if afternoon mixing is slightly stronger. Model consensus shows little day-to-day change through the weekend, keeping rain chances near zero and limiting any cooling influence from clouds or sea-breeze effects. Traders therefore assign the highest implied probability to the 92–93 °F bin, reflecting the narrow range of expected outcomes around the long-term June average high of 92 °F.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Recent forecasts from sources like CBS News and world-weather.info point to a high of 73–75°F at Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) on June 28, 2026, driven by a persistent marine layer and onshore flow from the Pacific.** This coastal moderation typically caps daytime peaks several degrees below inland valleys, aligning with the market's heavy weighting toward 70–71°F (49.5% implied probability) and nearby bins. 

Models show gradual afternoon clearing but limited warming due to low-level stratus and sea breezes, keeping the outcome near or slightly below seasonal averages. Earlier June heatwave conditions have eased, with current guidance emphasizing 3–6°F below-normal temperatures through the weekend. 

Traders appear to price in this stable marine influence and short-term model consensus, while acknowledging minor upside risk if clearing accelerates. Resolution hinges on the official National Weather Service daily climatological report for LAX maximum temperature.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Persistent marine layer influence and onshore flow are keeping San Francisco's June 28 high tightly clustered in the upper 60s to low 70s.**  

Official forecasts and recent model guidance indicate a deepened marine layer (around 2,000–2,500 ft) with patchy fog and drizzle potential, limiting afternoon warming under typical June "gloom" conditions. This setup favors a maximum near 68–71°F at downtown or airport stations, consistent with the market's two leading bins (22.5% and 21.5%).  

Differentiation among the close outcomes hinges on small variations in marine-layer depth, wind direction, and fog clearance timing: stronger or more persistent onshore flow and later burn-off cap highs near 68–69°F, while modest compression or brief clearing allows brief warming into the low 70s. No strong upper-level ridge or offshore flow is expected to push temperatures significantly higher, keeping 74°F+ outcomes at lower implied probability. Historical June averages near 67°F and current coastal water temperatures reinforce the cool bias. Traders are weighting the latest National Weather Service and ensemble guidance, which show limited day-to-day spread for this specific date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models from sources including SMN and international ensembles indicate a June 28 maximum in Mexico City near 22–24°C, with afternoon cloud cover and scattered showers expected to cap peaks near seasonal normals of roughly 24°C. Recent days have seen highs of 23–26°C under similar early-summer patterns, while model runs show minor spread tied to the timing and intensity of convective activity over the Valley of Mexico. These conditions produce closely matched market-implied odds across 22–24°C, as traders weigh the balance between clear intervals that could allow brief warming and widespread moisture that would suppress readings below 21°C or above 25°C. Updated model guidance and official observations tomorrow morning will refine resolution near the observed daily high.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent El Niño development, with Niño-3.4 indices rising above +1°C and strengthening through mid-2026, has tilted Panama toward drier, sunnier conditions that favor elevated daytime maxima. Typical June highs near 30°C can reach 32–34°C when reduced cloud cover and limited afternoon convection allow greater solar heating, while localized showers or stronger trades cap peaks closer to 32°C. Forecaster consensus on short-term model runs shows modest spread around these thresholds due to timing of tropical moisture and boundary-layer mixing, explaining the tight clustering of trader-implied probabilities near 32–34°C rather than a dominant outlier.
